scenario: Granite Bank runs Camunda 8.8 SaaS with 8500 transactions per day. After selecting the target flow node and clicking Apply Modification on 150 process instances, the operator now faces a confirmation modal that summarizes the selected modifications before any change is persisted on the cluster.
question: Which button in the confirmation modal actually starts the batch modification?

options.text: The Continue button starts the batch modification operation; it is the same button that earlier confirmed entering batch modification mode from the information modal
The Confirm button starts the batch modification operation, paired with typing the word MODIFY into a confirmation text box before the operator can submit
The Apply button in the summary modal starts the batch modification on the selected process instances at the chosen target flow node for the operation
The Move button starts the batch modification operation; it is the same button used earlier to enter batch modification mode from the process instance toolbar

a.text: Incorrect. Continue is used on the earlier information modal that confirms switching into batch mode; the summary modal that starts the operation uses an Apply button instead.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no MODIFY confirmation text box on the batch modification summary modal; that pattern belongs to the migration confirmation flow, not batch modification.

c.text: Correct. The batch modification guide says the operator clicks the Apply button on the summary modal to start the batch modification on the selected process instances at the chosen target flow node.

d.text: Incorrect. The Move button is the toolbar entry to batch mode; the actual commit happens through Apply Modification and Apply in the summary modal, not by clicking Move again.

explanation: Per the batch modification guide, after clicking Apply Modification a confirmation modal appears that summarizes the modifications, and clicking the Apply button on that summary modal starts the batch modification operation.
